task_completion.description: Rename existing track to 'Program' and create 'Narration' track
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour DAW timeline or mixer view, the track headers should clearly show one track named 'Program' and another track named 'Narration'.
An audio region/clip should be visible on the timeline specifically within the 'Narration' track.

task_completion.description: Position the narration clip to begin at exactly 10.0 seconds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left edge (start) of the audio clip on the 'Narration' track must align exactly with the 10.0-second mark on the timeline ruler at the top of the editor.

task_completion.description: Place a session marker named 'AD Start' at 10.0 seconds
task_completion.visual_evidence: A marker labeled 'AD Start' should be visible on the marker ruler/timeline at exactly the 10.0-second position.

task_completion.description: Duck the 'Program' track volume between 10.0 and 20.0 seconds
task_completion.visual_evidence: The volume automation lane for the 'Program' track should be visible, showing a clear dip (reduction in volume) that starts at 10.0 seconds and returns to normal around 20.0 seconds.

task_completion.description: Export the final stereo mix to the specified path and save the session
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Export dialog showing the file path '/home/ga/Audio/ife_mix/ad_mix.wav', or a file manager/terminal window showing the 'ad_mix.wav' file successfully created in the '/home/ga/Audio/ife_mix/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the audio editing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application open with active interactions (mouse cursors, open menus, drawn automation points) rather than terminal windows executing scripts to modify session files.

integrity.description: Ducking was achieved using DAW automation rather than replacing the audio file with a pre-processed one.
integrity.visual_evidence: Automation points and lines should be visibly drawn on the 'Program' track's automation lane in Ardour.

integrity.description: The final mix was exported using Ardour's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour 'Export' dialog should be visible in the screenshots, rather than CLI tools like ffmpeg or sox being used in a terminal to mix the files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Narration start time must be exactly 10.0 seconds (sample 441000) with a tolerance of 22050 samples. Ducking must reduce the 'Program' track volume by at least 6.0 dB during the 10.0s to 20.0s window (specifically checked at 15.0s compared to the pre-duck volume at 5.0s). The required export path is exactly '/home/ga/Audio/ife_mix/ad_mix.wav'.
